round_summary: All agents reaffirm restraint, coordinate on continued recovery before adding cows, and reinforce mutual trust without sanctions or defections.
Agents show strong coordination and trust-building around equitable, gradual herd growth and propose shared limits to prevent overuse.
Agents show restraint-oriented coordination with proposed conditional limits on future additions, though two agents still add cows simultaneously.
Agents reinforce a cooperative rotation norm of holding or pausing additions, with conditional conservation triggers if pasture decline accelerates.
Agents coordinate a staggered catch-up addition for Agent 0 while Agents 1 and 2 hold, reinforcing trust and a shared norm of spaced herd growth to protect the pasture.
All agents coordinate around a shared cap of 23 cows, emphasizing fairness, promise-keeping, and pasture protection.
All agents reaffirm the shared norm of equal herd sizes and restraint, coordinating continued cooperation to protect the pasture.
All agents reaffirm an equal-herd restraint norm at 23, emphasizing fairness, pasture health, and continued cooperative coordination.
All agents reaffirm a shared restraint norm of maintaining equal herds at 23 to preserve fairness and the commons.
All agents reaffirm a shared norm of equal herd sizes and mutual restraint to preserve the commons and maintain fairness.
